name: Douleur Neuropathique 4 Questions
description: A 10-item screening tool for neuropathic pain combining patient interview (7 items) and clinical examination (3 items)
instructions: This questionnaire is to be completed by the clinician. The first two questions are answered by the patient during the interview. The last two questions require a brief clinical examination of the painful area.

sections.name: Interview of the Patient
sections.description: Questions about pain characteristics and associated symptoms

sections.name: Examination of the Patient
sections.description: Clinical examination of the painful area

questions.groupLabel: Does the pain have one or more of the following characteristics?
questions.label: Burning

questions.groupLabel: Does the pain have one or more of the following characteristics?
questions.label: Painful cold

questions.groupLabel: Does the pain have one or more of the following characteristics?
questions.label: Electric shocks

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain associated with one or more of the following symptoms in the same area?
questions.label: Tingling

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain associated with one or more of the following symptoms in the same area?
questions.label: Pins and needles

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain associated with one or more of the following symptoms in the same area?
questions.label: Numbness

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain associated with one or more of the following symptoms in the same area?
questions.label: Itching

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain located in an area where the physical examination may reveal one or more of the following characteristics?
questions.label: Hypoesthesia to touch (decreased sensation to light touch)
questions.examinationMethod: Test with cotton wool or soft brush. Compare painful area to contralateral or adjacent normal area.

questions.groupLabel: Is the pain located in an area where the physical examination may reveal one or more of the following characteristics?
questions.label: Hypoesthesia to pinprick (decreased sensation to pinprick)
questions.examinationMethod: Test with a sharp object (e.g., wooden cocktail stick). Compare painful area to contralateral or adjacent normal area.

questions.groupLabel: In the painful area, can the pain be caused or increased by:
questions.label: Brushing (pain caused or increased by light brushing - allodynia)
questions.examinationMethod: Lightly brush the painful area with a soft brush, cotton wool, or finger. Record if this causes or increases pain.

scoringAlgorithm.calculation: Sum of all 10 items (each scored 0 or 1)

severityBands.label: Nociceptive Pain Likely
severityBands.description: Score below cutoff; neuropathic pain is unlikely. Pain is likely nociceptive in origin.

severityBands.label: Neuropathic Pain Likely
severityBands.description: Score at or above cutoff; neuropathic pain mechanisms are likely contributing. Consider neuropathic pain treatments.

riskRules.message: DN4 score ≥ 4 indicates neuropathic pain is likely. Consider neuropathic pain treatments (anticonvulsants, SNRIs, TCAs, topical agents).
Interview section score ≥ 3 suggests possible neuropathic component. Clinical examination recommended for confirmation.

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The DN4 (Douleur Neuropathique 4) is a validated screening tool for neuropathic pain. It combines patient-reported symptoms (7 items) with clinical examination findings (3 items). A score of 4 or more out of 10 has high sensitivity and specificity for identifying neuropathic pain.

cutoffExplanation.description: A cutoff of ≥4/10 provides optimal discrimination between nociceptive and neuropathic pain with good sensitivity and specificity.

interviewOnlyUse.description: The 7-item interview section (DN4-interview) can be used alone as a screening tool when clinical examination is not feasible

examinationNotes.hypoesthesia_touch: Reduced sensation to light touch compared to normal area indicates sensory loss
examinationNotes.hypoesthesia_pinprick: Reduced sensation to pinprick compared to normal area indicates sensory loss
examinationNotes.allodynia: Pain provoked by normally non-painful stimulus (light brushing) indicates central sensitization

treatmentImplications.nociceptive: Standard analgesics (NSAIDs, paracetamol, opioids) may be effective
treatmentImplications.neuropathic: Consider first-line neuropathic agents: pregabalin, gabapentin, duloxetine, amitriptyline. Topical lidocaine or capsaicin for localized pain.

actions.nociceptive: Neuropathic mechanisms unlikely. Treat with standard analgesic approaches appropriate to pain severity and cause.
actions.neuropathic: Neuropathic mechanisms likely. Initiate neuropathic pain treatment. Consider referral to pain specialist if refractory.

limitations: Mixed pain states may have both nociceptive and neuropathic components
May not capture all neuropathic pain presentations
references: Bouhassira D, Attal N, Alchaar H, et al. Comparison of pain syndromes associated with nervous or somatic lesions and development of a new neuropathic pain diagnostic questionnaire (DN4). Pain. 2005;114(1-2):29-36.
Bouhassira D, Attal N, Fermanian J, et al. Development and validation of the Neuropathic Pain Symptom Inventory. Pain. 2004;108(3):248-257.
